epigraph.text: Many are the plans in the mind of a man, but it is the purpose of the Lord that will stand.
epigraph.quote: The Kruskal-Wallis test extends rank-based methods to multiple groups.

content.text:
The Kruskal-Wallis test extends Mann-Whitney to compare more than 
two groups—a nonparametric alternative to one-way ANOVA.

content.title: Kruskal-Wallis Test
content.content:
1. Combine all observations and rank them

2. Calculate the sum of ranks for each group: $R_1, R_2, \ldots, R_k$

3. Compute the test statistic:
 \[
 H = \frac{12}{N(N+1)} \sum_{i=1}^k \frac{R_i^2}{n_i} - 3(N+1)
 \]

4. Under $H_0$, $H \approx \chi^2_{k-1}$ for large samples

items: Similar distribution shapes (to interpret as comparing medians)

content.text: Much weaker than ANOVA's normality assumption.

content.title: Kruskal-Wallis
content.problem:
Three regions' tree heights are compared. Rather than 
assume normality, the Kruskal-Wallis test ranks all heights combined 
and tests whether rank sums differ across regions.
